metadata.description: Short prefix for resource names (lowercase alphanumeric).
Hub region (Fabric capacity + private endpoint + resolver).
Entra tenant id for the Fabric Private Link service.
Fabric capacity administrator (a real Entra user UPN, or SP object id). Required.
Local administrator username for the VMs.
Local administrator password for the VMs.
Pre-shared key for the VNet-to-VNet VPN connections.
Deploy the Fabric tenant Private Link service + private endpoint. Requires tenant Azure Private Link enabled in the Fabric admin portal first.
Deploy Azure Bastion in both VNets (break-glass admin access).
Deny all Internet outbound on the VM/client/DNS subnets (stricter posture).
VM size for the lab VMs.
Static IP for the DNS Private Resolver inbound endpoint (inside snet-dnspr-inbound).
Static IP for the on-premises DNS server VM (inside snet-dns).

Public parent domains conditionally forwarded from on-prem to the resolver. Use broad parents (powerbi.com, fabric.microsoft.com) so the FULL Fabric/Power BI name resolution — portal, APIs, and OneLake (onelake.dfs/blob.fabric.microsoft.com, workspace FQDNs) — is sent to the private resolver. Narrow app.* forwarders leak OneLake to public DNS because its public CNAME chain resolves fully before the privatelink hop is re-queried.

Subnet definitions. Each item:
{
  name: string
  addressPrefix: string
  nsgId: string?            // associate an NSG
  delegation: string?       // service name, e.g. "Microsoft.Network/dnsResolvers"
  disablePeNetworkPolicies: bool?  // set true for private endpoint subnets
}
Custom DNS servers for the VNet. Empty = Azure-provided DNS.
Map of subnet name -> resource id.

Subnet definitions. Each item:
{
  name: string
  addressPrefix: string
  nsgId: string?            // associate an NSG
  delegation: string?       // service name, e.g. "Microsoft.Network/dnsResolvers"
  disablePeNetworkPolicies: bool?  // set true for private endpoint subnets
}
Custom DNS servers for the VNet. Empty = Azure-provided DNS.
Map of subnet name -> resource id.

Subnet definitions. Each item:
{
  name: string
  addressPrefix: string
  nsgId: string?            // associate an NSG
  delegation: string?       // service name, e.g. "Microsoft.Network/dnsResolvers"
  disablePeNetworkPolicies: bool?  // set true for private endpoint subnets
}
Custom DNS servers for the VNet. Empty = Azure-provided DNS.
Map of subnet name -> resource id.
Hub VNet resource id to link the zones to.
Private DNS zone names. Default = the three tenant-level Fabric/Power BI zones.
Array of { name, id } for each zone, for a private endpoint DNS zone group.
Azure region. Must match the VNet region.
Inbound endpoint subnet id (delegated to Microsoft.Network/dnsResolvers).
Static private IP for the inbound endpoint (must be inside the inbound subnet).
Configured static inbound endpoint IP (use as the on-prem conditional-forwarder target).
Capacity administrators (Entra user UPNs or service principal object ids). Required.
Deploy the tenant Private Link service + private endpoint. Requires tenant Azure Private Link to be enabled in the Fabric admin portal first.
Entra tenant id used for the Private Link service.
Private DNS zone configs [{ name, id }] for the endpoint DNS zone group.
VM name (also used as computer name; keep <= 15 chars).
Subnet resource id for the NIC.
Per-NIC DNS servers. Set to ["168.63.129.16"] for the DNS server VM so it can resolve while it bootstraps. Empty = inherit from VNet.
Inline command for a Windows Custom Script Extension. Empty = no extension.
VM name (also used as computer name; keep <= 15 chars).
Subnet resource id for the NIC.
Per-NIC DNS servers. Set to ["168.63.129.16"] for the DNS server VM so it can resolve while it bootstraps. Empty = inherit from VNet.
Inline command for a Windows Custom Script Extension. Empty = no extension.
VM name (also used as computer name; keep <= 15 chars).
Subnet resource id for the NIC.
Per-NIC DNS servers. Set to ["168.63.129.16"] for the DNS server VM so it can resolve while it bootstraps. Empty = inherit from VNet.
Inline command for a Windows Custom Script Extension. Empty = no extension.

Gateway SKU. Must be an AZ SKU (VpnGw1AZ-VpnGw5AZ); non-AZ VpnGw1-5 SKUs are no longer allowed for new VPN gateways. AZ SKUs require the associated Standard public IP to have zones configured (set on the pip below).
BGP Autonomous System Number. Must be non-reserved (64512-65514 or 65521-65534) and differ between the two gateways for the VNet-to-VNet BGP session.

Gateway SKU. Must be an AZ SKU (VpnGw1AZ-VpnGw5AZ); non-AZ VpnGw1-5 SKUs are no longer allowed for new VPN gateways. AZ SKUs require the associated Standard public IP to have zones configured (set on the pip below).
BGP Autonomous System Number. Must be non-reserved (64512-65514 or 65521-65534) and differ between the two gateways for the VNet-to-VNet BGP session.

resources.name: [format('{0}/to-{1}', parameters('spokeVnetName'), parameters('hubVnetName'))]
dependsOn: [resourceId('Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/virtualNetworkPeerings', split(format('{0}/to-{1}', parameters('hubVnetName'), parameters('spokeVnetName')), '/')[0], split(format('{0}/to-{1}', parameters('hubVnetName'), parameters('spokeVnetName')), '/')[1])]

metadata.description: Shared key used by both connections.
Run on the on-prem client VM to confirm private resolution.
